commands.review_summary:
The PR adds explicit `--persist` handling for reply/session preference commands so selected usage, model, and thinking settings can be marked for persistence across session rollovers.

PR surface: Source +179, Tests +156. Total +335 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: after `/usage full --persist`, a later `/usage tokens` without `--persist` updates `responseUsage` but leaves `persistentPreferenceFields` containing `responseUsage`, so the later supposedly session-scoped value can still roll over.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a contributor-visible code fix plus real behavior proof before merge; ClawSweeper should not queue repair markers while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: feat(reply): persist session preferences
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Some reply preferences currently behave like transcript-local session state. When a session rolls over after a daily or idle boundary, settings such as `/usage full` disappear even though users often intend them as durable reply preferences. That makes usage/footer output inconsistent after rollover.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds explicit `--persist` support to preference commands. `/usage`, `/thinking`, and `/model` can mark the corresponding setting as durable in `persistentPreferenceFields`; rollover preserves only explicitly persisted fields, so existing non-persistent behavior remains unchanged. The flag is stripped from agent-visible directive text and is not applied to unrelated controls.

## User Impact

Users can run `/usage full --persist`, `/thinking high --persist`, or `/model <id> --persist` to keep those preferences across session rollovers. Users who omit `--persist` keep the existing temporary, session-scoped behavior.

## Evidence

Real behavior covered by focused tests:

- `/usage full --persist` writes `responseUsage: "full"` plus `persistentPreferenceFields: ["responseUsage"]`.
- Daily rollover preserves persisted `responseUsage`.
- Daily rollover drops non-persisted `responseUsage`, so the default behavior is unchanged.
- `/think high --persist` and `/model openai/gpt-4o --persist` store the persistent marker and strip `--persist` from agent-visible text.
- Ordinary messages containing `--persist` remain unchanged.

Validated locally on 2026-06-20:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply.directive.parse.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts
```

Result:

- `src/auto-reply/reply.directive.parse.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`
- 2 Vitest shards passed.
- 4 related test files passed.
- 240 tests passed.

Additional validation already run on this branch:

```bash
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply.directive.parse.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts src/config/sessions/persistent-preferences.ts src/config/sessions/types.ts src/config/sessions/store-load.ts src/plugins/session-entry-slot-keys.ts
git diff --check
```
